title: pouteria ovatostipulata
intro: Pouteria ovatostipulata, also known as the Ceylon olive tree, is a plant species native to Sri Lanka. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities. The leaves and bark of the Pouteria ovatostipulata have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these uses.
